text: The surgeon places the first incision around the belly button, followed by three smaller incisions, each approximately eight millimeters in size, on the left and right sides of the abdomen.

The video shows a sped-up view of the surgical field, with the hernia visibly exposed in the center of the image. The surgeon dissects surrounding anatomy to clearly visualize the hernia and its anatomical context.
The surgeon identifies the hernia defect and cleans the area before placing a mesh inside. The mesh is unrolled to cover the entire defect, utilizing a new lightweight and strong material.
The surgeon displays a pro grip mesh, highlighting its design that allows it to adhere without the need for sutures or tacks.
The surgeon demonstrates the placement of a pro grip mesh with a velcro-like texture that adheres to the tissue, ensuring secure coverage of the hernia defect.
The surgeon places the mesh over the hernia site, having removed the hernia sac. The cover of the hernia is being closed, while adhesions are being carefully freed.
